# Introduction

This User Guide is for platform and application teams consuming Consul Enterprise capabilities after the platform has been installed and handed over for operation. It covers service discovery, the service catalog, service mesh and gateways, multi-infrastructure service connectivity, deployment automation, and services with Consul client agents.

<Note>

Operator topics such as ACL governance, namespaces, admin partitions, cluster peering setup, observability, backup, restore, upgrades, and support readiness are covered in the [Consul Administration Guide](/validated-designs/consul/administration-guide/introduction). Deployment architecture and install-time setup are covered in the [Consul Installation Guide](/validated-designs/consul/installation-guide/introduction).

</Note>

<Warning> Please note that this document is a work in progress and is subject to change. </Warning>

HashiCorp Validated Designs (HVDs) provide prescriptive guidance curated from our experience supporting numerous customer journeys with Consul Enterprise.

## Use cases covered

This guide covers the Consul Enterprise capabilities that platform and application teams consume once the platform is installed and operational:

| Use case | Summary |
| --- | --- |
| [Multi-platform service discovery](/validated-designs/consul/user-guide/multi-platform-service-discovery) | Register services, configure health checks, and use the Consul service catalog across platforms. |
| [Service discovery catalog](/validated-designs/consul/user-guide/service-discovery-catalog) | Use DNS discovery and prepared queries to discover registered Consul services. |
| [Service mesh and gateways](/validated-designs/consul/user-guide/service-mesh-and-gateways) | Use Consul service mesh, gateways, and API gateways with HashiCups examples. |
| [Multi-platform application deployment](/validated-designs/consul/user-guide/multi-platform-application-deployment) | Deploy example applications across Kubernetes, EC2, and Nomad using Consul. |
| [Scale services on multiple infrastructures](/validated-designs/consul/user-guide/scale-services-on-multiple-infrastructures) | Use Consul service mesh, mesh gateways, DNS, and catalog sync across multiple infrastructures. |
| [Services with Consul client agents](/validated-designs/consul/user-guide/services-with-consul-client-agents) | Deploy services with Consul client agents on VMs using immutable infrastructure and CI/CD. |
| [Deployment automation](/validated-designs/consul/user-guide/deployment-automation) | Automate Consul-related delivery workflows with CI/CD, GitOps, and infrastructure tooling. |
